title: set/frozenset: a shared set iterator double-DECREFs si_set in the exhaustion path -> use-after-free (setiter_iternext, Objects/setobject.c:1131)
description: setiter_iternext (Objects/setobject.c) reads `PySetObject *so = si->si_set` unguarded (:1101), takes only the SET's critical section around the table scan (Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION(so), :1116-1127), and then on exhaustion runs `si->si_set = NULL; Py_DECREF(so);` (:1130-1131) OUTSIDE that section. Two threads advancing the SAME set iterator to exhaustion both read the same non-NULL so and both Py_DECREF(so) -> the set's refcount is dropped twice -> use-after-free. This is the set-iterator sibling of the dict-iterator double-DECREF TSAN-0053, and the memory-safety CRASH face of the value-benign set-iterator data race TSAN-0040 (cpython#144356).
signature_note: No race signature emitted (tsanNOPARSE crash: negative-refcount abort / GC assert / segfault, not a TSan data-race report). Its TSan-race face is the value-benign si_pos cursor race already carried by TSAN-0040 (race:setiter_iternext / setiter_len, cpython#144356); adding a signature here would collide with TSAN-0040.
sites: Objects/setobject.c setiter_iternext:1130-1131 -- `si->si_set = NULL; Py_DECREF(so);` non-atomically drops the iterator's one owning ref to the set; OUTSIDE the Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION(so) that ends at :1127. Two concurrent next() callers both do it.
Objects/setobject.c setiter_iternext:1101 -- `PySetObject *so = si->si_set;` plain read of the reference the exhaustion path clears+decrefs
race_kind: shared iterator exhaustion: non-atomic clear+DECREF of si->si_set (the iterator's single owning reference to the set) across threads -> double-DECREF / use-after-free (refcount underflow). Memory-unsafe (crosses the gh-124397 'must not crash' bar). The set analogue of TSAN-0053 (dict iterator); distinct from the value-benign si_pos cursor race TSAN-0040.
shared_state: setiterobject.si_set (the owning reference to the source set/frozenset) on a single shared set iterator; the referenced set's refcount
severity: HIGH (CRASHES): the double-DECREF drives the set's refcount below zero. repro.py (8 threads sharing one set iterator, refilled on exhaustion) aborts ~8/8 on debug-ft-nojit with `_Py_NegativeRefcount` at setobject.c:1131, and SEGVs/aborts 6/6 (core dumped) on release-ft-nojit-o0 (genuine UAF, not a debug tripwire). On a LONG-LIVED shared frozenset (module-level constant, refcount>1) the underflow is silent until the next gc.collect(): `Python/gc_free_threading.c:999 update_refs Assertion refcount>=0` on a `frozenset` with a wild refcount (~2^60), or a SIGSEGV UAF on a later access. Reachable from pure Python; realistic for a shared module-level frozenset iterated from threads. Free-threaded build only.
repro_secondary: repro_frozenset_gc.py (long-lived frozenset -> GC 'refcount too small' / segfault face)
repro_reliability: PLAIN free-threaded builds, no sanitizer: debug-ft-nojit ~8/8 within seconds (`_Py_NegativeRefcount` @ setobject.c:1131); release-ft-nojit-o0 6/6 SIGSEGV/SIGABRT core-dumped. Long-lived frozenset face (repro_frozenset_gc.py) aborts in validate/update_refs or SEGVs. Surfaced by fusil --tsan un-masking fleet-15 as the `frozenset` GC-assert NOPARSE cluster (2 veh, mod=copy) -- the module is incidental; iter(frozenset) returns a set_iterator over the shared frozenset.
suggested_fix: Serialize concurrent next() on the SAME iterator and drop the ref exactly once. cpython#144357 (OPEN PR by the #144356 author) already does this: it widens the lock to Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION2(self, so) and, under Py_GIL_DISABLED, makes exhaustion sticky via si_pos=-1 and REMOVES the `si_set = NULL; Py_DECREF(so)` from iternext entirely (the set ref is dropped only in dealloc). That PR fixes this crash but has been stalled since 2026-05.
prior_art: cpython#144356 ('Data race in set iterator length_hint under no-gil', OPEN) is the value-benign DATA-RACE face (= our TSAN-0040), with an OPEN fix PR cpython#144357 that widens setiter_iternext to CRITICAL_SECTION2(self, so) and removes the exhaustion decref under FT -- which fixes THIS crash too, but the PR has been stalled since 2026-05. Older CLOSED #112069/#117935 ('Make setiter_iternext thread-safe') hardened the value path but left the si_set double-DECREF. So the memory-safety CRASH is unfiled as such: #144356 describes only the data race. Best move = corroborate on #144356/#144357 with the crash reproducer (demonstrating memory-unsafety, not just a benign race) to unstall the fix -- mirrors the dict case (TSAN-0053/#154130) except that issue was CLOSED so we filed new, whereas #144356 is OPEN with an active fix.
confirmed_build: debug-ft-nojit (CPython 3.16.0a0, --disable-gil, main@a1d580430c8); SEGVs on release-ft-nojit-o0 -> NOT debug-only
confirmed_unfixed_on: 3.16.0a0 (fleet build main@a1d580430c8; fix PR #144357 open/unmerged since 2026-05)
related: Set analogue of the dict-iterator double-DECREF TSAN-0053 (dictobject.c dictiter_iternext_threadsafe fail path). Same 'shared iterator exhaustion double-frees the container' shape. Distinct from TSAN-0040 (value-benign set-iter cursor race). frozenset is the long-lived face (GC-caught) just as frozendict is for TSAN-0053.
notes: Un-masking fleet-15 surfaced it as a `frozenset` GC-assert (validate/update_refs 'refcount too small'), NOPARSE. iter(frozenset)/iter(set) => set_iterator; the exhaustion double-DECREF is the same bug the dict iterator has (TSAN-0053). #144357 (open, stalled) fixes it; corroborate there rather than filing new (the issue is OPEN). Two crash signatures: throwaway set -> immediate _Py_NegativeRefcount @ setobject.c:1131; long-lived frozenset -> GC 'refcount too small' or SIGSEGV UAF. Outward-facing; awaiting go-ahead to post the corroboration.
